commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: The verse could be taken as addressed to the whole of Israel, not citing Judah and Ephraim separately, but as though addressing a unit comprising every tribe, since every tribe of Israel was led out of the land of Egypt.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Family, including all the posterity of Jacob. (Worthington)
He afterwards addresses the ten tribes in particular. (St. Jerome) (Calmet)
